message:
fix(combinator): bulkhead cancel releases granted-but-unclaimed permits

bulkhead.rs:
- Fix cancel_entry to detect and handle the case where a queued entry has
  already been granted a permit (result == Some(Ok(()))) but the caller has
  not yet claimed it via check_entry. Previously, cancelling such an entry
  only marked it as cancelled without releasing the permit back to the pool,
  causing a permanent permit leak — the available count decreased by 1 for
  each cancelled-after-grant entry, eventually starving the bulkhead
- When a granted permit is cancelled: call release_permit to return the
  weight, remove the entry entirely from the queue (no need to keep a
  cancelled+resolved entry), and increment the cancelled metric
- Add test that exhausts permits, enqueues a waiter, grants the permit via
  process_queue, then cancels before claiming — verifying available() returns
  to 1 after cancellation

circuit_breaker.rs:
- Add test documenting that panicking inside CircuitBreaker::call() leaks
  the probe permit in HalfOpen state. The test trips the breaker, panics
  during a probe via catch_unwind, and verifies probes_active remains at 1
  (leaked) and subsequent probes are rejected with HalfOpenFull. This is a
  known-bug regression test, not a fix — the probe guard needs Drop cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
